title: Romulea pylia
taxonomy: Romulea pylia belongs to the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves, which are similar to those of other irises. Romulea pylia is further classified as a species within the genus Romulea, which comprises a group of small, flowering plants native to southern Africa. The genus Romulea is typically classified within the tribe Irideae, which is a subgroup of the family Iridaceae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between Romulea pylia and other plants within the same family.

intro: Romulea pylicantha, commonly known as Romulea pylia, is a small, bulbous plant species belonging to the family Iridaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Romulea pylia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
